recent_commits.message:
feat: Smart Reallocation dashboard with live backend analytics

- Add /api/analytics/smart-reallocation endpoint with median-based surplus/deficit detection
- Build SmartReallocationDashboard with KPIs, expandable recommendations, before/after utilization bars
- Charts: department utilization, state status pie, state allocation vs spending
- Surplus/deficit department comparison tables
- Add getSmartReallocation to api.js
- Update App.jsx routing to use new dashboard
recent_commits.summary: The commit introduces a new feature for the Admin Dashboard, which includes an Anomaly Detection Dashboard. This dashboard provides real-time analytics on anomalies using various methods such as Isolation Forest and Z-Score. The dashboard displays key performance indicators (KPIs), expandable recommendations, before/after utilization bars, charts showing department utilization, state status pie, and state allocation vs spending. It also includes surplus/deficit department comparison tables.

recent_commits.message:
feat: add CSV upload, ML anomaly detection, and fund lapse prediction

Backend:
- POST /api/upload-csv: CSV file upload with validation and auto-insert
- GET /api/upload/template-columns: returns required column schema
- GET /api/analytics/anomaly-detection: multi-model detection
  (Isolation Forest + Z-Score + Rule Engine) with composite scoring
- GET /api/analytics/fund-lapse-prediction: Linear Regression forecasting
  with district/dept-level risk scoring and trend analysis
- Fix: GEMINI_API_KEY made optional, emoji prints replaced for Windows

Frontend:
- CSVUploadPage: drag-drop CSV upload with preview and validation
- AnomalyDetectionDashboard: severity charts, method comparison,
  dept/state breakdown, expandable detail rows
- PredictiveModelingDashboard: spending trend with forecast overlay,
  department lapse chart, district risk table with factors
- Sidebar: added Upload Data + shield icon for Anomaly Detection
- api.js: 4 new endpoint functions

recent_commits.message:
Feature: Insights screen - Budget Utilization, Fund Lapse Risk, Reallocation Suggestions

- Analytics tab renamed to 'Insights' with 3 sub-tabs
- Tab 1 (Utilization): distribution overview + state-wise progress bars
- Tab 2 (Fund Lapse): at-risk depts with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW badges + unspent amounts
- Tab 3 (Reallocation): surplus->deficit arrow cards with suggested Cr amounts
- Backend: /api/fund-lapse-risk (adaptive threshold based on dataset median)
- Backend: /api/reallocation-suggestions (surplus/deficit dept pairs)
recent_commits.summary: The code adds three new API endpoints for fund lapse risk and reallocation suggestions. It also updates the UI to include a new tab in the Analytics section of the app, which displays utilization data, fund lapse risk information, and reallocation suggestions.

final_review: The developers built several features and systems for various applications, including an Admin Dashboard with Anomaly Detection, Insights, Fund Lapse Risk, Reallocation, and Leakage Map tabs in the AnalyticsScreen. They used React, Next.js, MongoDB, Express, and FastAPI technologies. Their development pattern involved frequent commits and well-structured code, with a focus on building working features rather than just adding setup/configurations. The code quality was assessed based on scores: 13+ for strong technical code, 7-12 for building working features, and 1-6 for mostly setup/configurations. AI-generated code portions were noted where multiple commits had generic descriptions or identical patterns. Standout commits included the BudgetGptScreen class with an API route for sending messages to a backend service, and the Admin Dashboard with Anomaly Detection. Wasted commits were identified as merge commits and empty files. The project was functional but could have been improved in terms of code quality and documentation. The overall verdict was that they built substantial features using React, Next.js, MongoDB, Express, and FastAPI technologies, with a focus on building working features rather than just adding setup/configurations.
